steps.title: Identify the constraint equation and x-intercept
steps.content: At the $x$-intercept on the graph, $y = 0$ (no K-type stars). The mass equation $m_M \cdot x + m_K \cdot y = 127,882$ becomes $m_M \cdot x = 127,882$. This allows us to find the mass of each M-star directly from the intercept.

steps.title: Solve for M-star mass using the intercept
steps.content: From the graph, the $x$-intercept is at $x \approx 160$. Therefore: $$m_M \cdot 160 = 127,882$$ $$m_M = \frac{127,882}{160} \approx 799.26$$ This is closest to choice A — A is correct.
